content.problemStatement:
Vacations have arrived and Chef wants to go to his home in ChefLand. There are two types of routes he can take:


Take a flight from his college to ChefArina which takes XXX minutes and then take a bus from ChefArina to ChefLand which takes YYY minutes.
Take a direct train from his college to ChefLand which takes ZZZ minutes.
Which of these two options is faster?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The first line of the test case contains three space-separated integers XXX, YYY, and ZZZ.


Output Format
For each test case, if Chef takes the train output `TRAIN`, if Chef takes the plane and the bus output `PLANEBUS`, if both are equal output `EQUAL`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `train`, `tRAiN`, `TrAin`, and `TRAIN` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers X, Y and Z.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: TRAIN, PLANEBUS or EQUAL, according to which travel option is faster (or if they take the same time).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 10+12=22 > 11, so TRAIN is faster.\nTest case 2: 3+5=8 < 15, so PLANEBUS is faster.\nTest case 3: 9+4=13 equals 13, so both are equal.
5+5=10 equals Z, therefore output is EQUAL.
First case: 1+2=3 < 5 → PLANEBUS. Second case: 10^9+10^9=2000000000 > 1999999999 → TRAIN.

solution.approach: For each test case compute sum = X + Y. Compare sum with Z. If sum < Z, the plane+bus option is faster → output "PLANEBUS". If sum > Z, the train is faster → output "TRAIN". If sum == Z, both take the same time → output "EQUAL". Use 64‑bit integers to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
